findings.claim: The q2 rationale presents a passage phrase in single quotes as if verbatim, but it is a stitched/elided quote that does not appear verbatim in the passage.
findings.evidence: q2 rationale: «...just den gräns som texten uttryckligen tvivlar på ('mindre säker på att gränsen håller'; ...)». The passage reads: «...desto mindre säker blir jag på att gränsen håller.» The quoted string 'mindre säker på att gränsen håller' omits the interposed 'blir jag' with no ellipsis marker, so it is not a verbatim passage substring (verified: NOT-IN-PASSAGE).
findings.why: The integrated read's stated standard is that every quoted phrase must appear verbatim in the current passage/option text. This is a genuine miss against that standard, but the quote is a faithful compression of a real sentence with no meaning change, no self-contradiction, and no reconciliation failure, so it does not overturn the CONSISTENT verdict — a minor metadata/quote-hygiene note only.
